text.title: AI Triage, Classify, and Route Inbound Issues Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the tracker-ai-triage-classify-route methodology plus the `skip-this-methodology` safety rule. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Triage MUST execute the steps in this fixed order: classify → severity → dedupe → label → route → assign. Skipping or reordering steps is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Order matters: dedupe before route avoids assigning duplicates; severity before SLA avoids arming timers on noise.

rule.statement: Severity = `blocker` MUST require explicit on-call confirmation before the SLA timer arms. Other severities are auto-routed.
rule.rationale: AI calling everything a blocker erodes the rotation; gating on the on-call confirmation keeps blocker scarcity intact.

rule.statement: Deduplication MUST use title+body embedding cosine similarity ≥ 0.85 against the last 1000 issues. Matches are linked, not closed automatically.
rule.rationale: Auto-close on similarity has a non-zero false-positive rate that lost issues; linking lets a human confirm.

rule.statement: The triage agent MUST emit one comment listing every classification it applied (type, severity, labels, route, assignee) so the assignee can dispute any field with a one-click reaction.
rule.rationale: Silent triage erodes trust; the visible audit comment is what makes AI triage acceptable to engineers.

rule.statement: Team routing MUST be derived from the matched CODEOWNERS entry, not from AI inference. AI suggests, CODEOWNERS decides.
rule.rationale: AI-inferred routing drifts from org structure within months; CODEOWNERS is the version-controlled source of truth.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the preconditions in `## Applies If` are not all true, or any item in `## Skip If` holds. The decision tree routes to this rule on those branches.
rule.rationale: A methodology that runs on the wrong input wastes tokens and produces misleading artifacts. The explicit skip rule is the safety valve.
